task_completion.description: Create the required layers with specified colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD Layer List panel should display the following layers with their respective colors: GROUND (brown), CASING (red), SCREEN (green), GRAVEL (yellow), WATER (cyan or blue), PUMP (magenta), SEAL (gray), DIMENSIONS (white), and TEXT (white).

task_completion.description: Draw the ground surface line and the main well casing
task_completion.visual_evidence: A wide brown horizontal line at the top (Y=0) representing the ground, and two long red vertical lines close together (X=-4 and X=4) extending downwards from the ground line to a capped bottom (Y=-150).

task_completion.description: Draw the well screen with perforations and the gravel pack
task_completion.visual_evidence: Green vertical lines inside the bottom portion of the casing (Y=-120 to -150) with small tick marks representing perforations. Yellow vertical boundary lines outside the lower casing (X=-10 and X=10, Y=-100 to -150) that are closed at the top and bottom.

task_completion.description: Draw the static/pumping water levels and the bentonite seal
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two cyan or blue horizontal lines crossing the casing (at Y=-40 and Y=-80). Gray vertical lines outside the top portion of the casing (X=-8 and X=8) extending from the ground down to Y=-25.
A magenta rectangle located inside the casing, positioned below the lower water level line (centered at Y=-100).

task_completion.description: Add vertical linear dimensions for key depths
task_completion.visual_evidence: White vertical dimension lines with arrows and text indicating the depths of 25 (Seal), 40 (Static Level), 100 (Gravel Top), and 150 (Total Depth) alongside the well structure.

task_completion.description: Add required text labels and save the file
task_completion.visual_evidence: White text annotations pointing to the respective well components ('GROUND SURFACE', 'STATIC WATER LEVEL', 'PUMPING WATER LEVEL', 'CASING', 'SCREEN', 'GRAVEL PACK', 'BENTONITE SEAL', 'SUBMERSIBLE PUMP') and a main title 'IRRIGATION WELL DETAIL'. The LibreCAD window title or command line history should indicate the file is saved as 'water_well_section.dxf'.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreCAD GUI to draw the well section
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreCAD interface with active drawing tools, command line inputs for CAD operations (like 'line', 'rect', 'dim'), or layer management, rather than a terminal window running a script.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually write DXF code in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing should be constructed visually within LibreCAD. There should be no evidence of the agent opening a text editor (like nano or vim) to write raw DXF markup.

integrity.description: Drawing consists of genuine CAD entities
integrity.visual_evidence: The elements on screen should be selectable CAD vectors (lines, dimensions, text blocks) native to LibreCAD, not an imported raster image of a well.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The drawing must be conceptually scaled where 1mm = 1ft. The total depth is 150 units (Y=-150). Key coordinates to verify visually: Ground is at Y=0. Casing lines are at X=±4. Screen lines are at X=±3 from Y=-120 to -150. Gravel pack boundaries are at X=±10 from Y=-100 to -150. Water levels are at Y=-40 and Y=-80. Pump is a rectangle at Y=-100. Seal lines are at X=±8 from Y=0 to -25. Dimensions must explicitly show the numbers 25, 40, 100, and 150. Required layers: GROUND, CASING, SCREEN, GRAVEL, WATER, PUMP, SEAL, DIMENSIONS, TEXT.
